title: Batch order refill in fast tick
problem_detected: Fast refill places missing grid orders serially — one API call per missing slot through the order router (25s timeout each). When 5 orders fill simultaneously in a volatile move, the grid is incomplete for 25-125 seconds.
evidence: grid_bot_service.py:11081-11294 — fast refill calls _place_slot_order() individually
bybit_client.py:1895-2028 — create_batch_orders() supports 20 orders per POST
order_router_service.py:155 — default timeout 25s per order
grid_bot_service.py:4012-4063 — _submit_batch_orders() used only for planned opening
proposed_improvement: Collect all missing slot orders during fast refill, submit as single batch API call via create_batch_orders(). Fall back to serial if batch fails.
why_now: Direct profit opportunity missed on every volatile multi-fill event.
notes_for_evaluator: Verify router serialization guarantees with batch.
evaluator_summary: SCOPE NARROWED after verification: Serial refill only affects neutral_classic_bybit mode. Other modes (neutral dynamic, scalp, long, short) already use _submit_batch_orders() in fast refill. Still valuable since neutral_classic is the primary mode used. Design question: batch bypasses router or router gets batch-aware path.
overlap_notes: None.
challenge_notes: Router serialization must be preserved. Partial-accept handling needed.
next_step: Design batch-aware router path, then implement.

title: Exchange-side TP/SL orders for crash protection
problem_detected: TP is not set exchange-side. SL IS already set via stop_loss_service.py → set_trading_stop().
evidence: take_profit_service.py — calculates tp_pct but doesn't place exchange order
stop_loss_service.py:327 — DOES call set_trading_stop for SL
bybit_client.py:2474 — set_trading_stop method exists and works
grid_bot_service.py:23298 — calls stop_loss_service when auto_stop_loss_enabled
proposed_improvement: Add exchange-side TP via set_trading_stop. SL already works — only TP gap remains.
why_now: TP gap means crash protection relies on bot-side trailing TP only.
notes_for_evaluator: SCOUT CLAIM PARTIALLY INCORRECT — SL already set exchange-side.
evaluator_summary: Scout claimed ZERO protection — WRONG. SL IS set via stop_loss_service. Only TP gap remains. Narrower scope than presented. Need to verify: auto_stop_loss_enabled default, SL update failure rate, trailing TP sync complexity. Downgraded from scout's HIGH to MEDIUM impact.
overlap_notes: SL already implemented. Only TP gap remains.
challenge_notes: Exchange TP must sync with trailing TP recalculations. Stale TP risk. Double-execution on reduce-only exits.
next_step: Verify auto_stop_loss_enabled default. Audit SL failure rate. Then scope to TP-only.

title: Include funding fees in daily loss calculation
problem_detected: Daily drawdown uses equity only. Funding fees tracked but excluded from kill-switch formula. Blind spot in trending markets.
evidence: risk_manager_service.py:222-226 — daily_drawdown_pct uses equity only
pnl_service.py:341-357 — known_funding_total computed but not in risk formula
proposed_improvement: Include accrued funding in daily loss formula. Verify whether equity already captures settled funding to avoid double-counting.
why_now: 0.1-0.3% funding per 8h is significant for small accounts.
notes_for_evaluator: Verify equity vs settled funding double-counting.
evaluator_summary: Real safety blind spot. Key question: does Bybit equity already include settled funding? If yes, only accrued-unsettled is the gap (estimate from rate * value * time). If no, include full funding. Complements funding rate harvesting feature.
overlap_notes: Complements funding rate harvesting (2026-03-16).
challenge_notes: Double-counting risk if equity includes settled funding. Per-bot funding needs position attribution.
next_step: Verify Bybit equity response contents. Implement accordingly.

title: Intermediate drawdown response stages
problem_detected: Binary kill-switch: full aggression until -8%, then full shutdown. No intermediate stages.
evidence: risk_manager_service.py:195-244 — only MAX_DAILY_LOSS_PCT threshold
proposed_improvement: Three stages at -4%/-6%/-8% with sizing reduction, entry pause, kill-switch.
why_now: Standard professional risk practice.
evaluator_summary: Good concept but needs design doc first. Hysteresis critical — without it, equity oscillating around -4% causes stage flapping. On $35 account, -4% = $1.40 — may be too sensitive. Must compose with quality sizing (SCOUT-011). DEFERRED until design addresses hysteresis, small-account calibration, mid-session propagation.
overlap_notes: Interacts with SCOUT-011 sizing. Must compose as multipliers.
challenge_notes: Hysteresis, small-account thresholds, mid-session sizing changes.
next_step: Write design doc first.

title: Portfolio correlation risk monitor
problem_detected: Correlated assets treated as independent risk units.
evidence: risk_manager_service.py:707-722 — independent per-symbol checks
proposed_improvement: Correlation clustering with exposure caps.
why_now: Correlated cascade risk.
evaluator_summary: Over-engineered for 3-5 bot small-capital system. Pearson correlation overkill. SIMPLER: static sector map + max-sector-exposure cap. Achieves 80% value at 20% cost. btc_correlation_filter already exists but disabled for key modes — fix that first.
overlap_notes: btc_correlation_filter exists, just disabled.
challenge_notes: Use static sector map, not dynamic correlation.
next_step: If pursued, implement as static sector map. No dynamic correlation.

title: Comprehensive operator alert system
problem_detected: Only direction change triggers alerts. No kill-switch, liquidation, or error alerts.
evidence: app_v5.js:810-855 — direction change only
proposed_improvement: Tiered alert system: CRITICAL/WARNING/INFO.
why_now: Missing critical event notifications.
evaluator_summary: Scope too broad. 3 tiers with 8+ alert types = large feature + alert fatigue. RECOMMEND: Phase A only — kill-switch + error-state alerts (CRITICAL tier, 2 alert types). These are rare, high-impact, zero false-positive. Expand only if operator wants more.
overlap_notes: SCOUT-013 (stuck bot) feeds into this. Design together.
challenge_notes: Start minimal. Alert fatigue kills value.
next_step: Scope to Phase A: kill-switch + error alerts only.

title: Auto-pilot rotation phased entry ramp
problem_detected: Full-size grid opens immediately on rotation. Buy-the-top risk.
evidence: auto_pilot_mixin.py — no confirmation after rotation
proposed_improvement: 3-phase entry ramp with state tracking.
why_now: Every rotation is a potential buy-the-top.
evaluator_summary: Problem is real but solution over-engineered. SIMPLER: 2-candle (30min) price stability check after rotation. If price moved >1.5% in either direction, skip. No phase tracking, no partial grids. Need log evidence of buy-the-top frequency before implementing.
overlap_notes: SCOUT-012 (5m confirmation) partially addresses this.
challenge_notes: Analyze rotation logs for buy-the-top pattern first.
next_step: Check rotation logs for post-rotation loss frequency.

title: Entry gate: spread width check
problem_detected: No spread validation at entry decision time. Spread can exceed grid step = negative-EV.
evidence: entry_gate_service.py — no spread check
neutral_grid_service.py:1398 — spread check only during order placement
proposed_improvement: Block entry when spread > 2x grid_step. Scope to spread only (drop depth/liquidation).
why_now: Wide spread = every fill underwater.
evaluator_summary: Good idea, scope reduced. Spread check is high-value, low-complexity. Block when spread > 2x effective_grid_step. Existing spread logic in neutral_grid_service can be refactored to share. Drop depth and liquidation checks (Phase 2 if needed).
overlap_notes: Complements SCOUT-009 (fee-aware). Both prevent negative-EV entries.
challenge_notes: Use grid_step as baseline, not average spread.
next_step: Implement spread check in entry_gate_service.

title: Fee-aware grid level pre-filtering
problem_detected: Fee guard fires AFTER grid construction, expands step instead of reducing levels. 0.148% step < 0.16% fee floor = systematic loss per fill.
evidence: grid_bot_service.py:14916-14927 — fee guard post-construction
strategy_config.py — GRID_STEP_MAJOR_MULT=0.4
strategy_config.py:1472 — FAST_EXEC_TAKER_FEE_RATE=0.00055 hardcoded
proposed_improvement: Move fee guard to pre-construction. Reduce level count if step < fee floor. Add expected_profit_per_fill estimate. Block if estimate < 0.
why_now: Systematic negative-EV leak compounding over hundreds of fills/day.
evaluator_summary: DOWNGRADED after verification. FEE_AWARE_MIN_STEP_ENABLED=True and the guard DOES fire, expanding step to 0.26% (not 0.16% as scout claimed — scout missed 0.10% slippage buffer). Fills are NOT negative-EV. The real issue is optimization: expanding step vs reducing levels. Reducing levels would keep tighter spacing with fewer orders, potentially improving fill rate. Valid improvement but NOT a systematic loss — each fill IS profitable after the guard fires.
overlap_notes: Complements SCOUT-008 (spread check).
challenge_notes: Level reduction must respect min-notional. Edge case, not blocker.
next_step: Move fee guard to pre-construction. Implement immediately.

title: Daily trend filter for directional entries
problem_detected: No daily trend context in entry decisions. Entries fight macro trend.
evidence: entry_readiness_service.py:1310-1313 — only 15m/1m
strategy_config.py:748-756 — MTF includes D but unused
proposed_improvement: Block directional entries >2% against daily EMA20. Cache daily candles 1h.
why_now: Directional mode stopouts from fighting macro trend.
evaluator_summary: Simple, defensible for directional modes. Low priority because directional mode usage frequency unclear. Connects existing MTF config to execution.
overlap_notes: Complementary to SCOUT-012 (5m neutral confirmation).
challenge_notes: Directional only. Configurable threshold per symbol.
next_step: Implement daily EMA20 check for long/short modes.

title: Setup quality score to position sizing mapping
problem_detected: Quality score (0-100) computed but doesn't influence sizing.
evidence: grid_bot_service.py:15474-15476 — flow_conf applied, not quality
entry_readiness_service.py — score is display-only
proposed_improvement: Map quality bands to sizing multipliers: <60 → 65%, 60-71 → 85%, 72+ → 100%. Lock at entry time.
why_now: Weak setups = same size as strong = unnecessary risk.
notes_for_evaluator: Lock at entry, not per cycle.
evaluator_summary: Clean, simple. Connects existing data to action. Lock multiplier at entry time. Don't apply to continuation adds. Must compose with other sizing modifiers as multiplicative factors.
overlap_notes: Interacts with SCOUT-004 (drawdown stages). Design as composable multipliers.
challenge_notes: Lock at entry, don't recalculate. Toggle for easy disable.
next_step: Add quality_sizing_multiplier at grid construction.

title: 5-minute confirmation gate for neutral scanner
problem_detected: Neutral scanner uses 15m ADX only. Misses 5m micro-trends.
evidence: neutral_scanner_service.py:808-880 — 15m ADX only
proposed_improvement: Add 5m price velocity check. Block if |velocity_5m| > 1.5%.
why_now: Micro-trend entries are primary neutral loss cause.
evaluator_summary: Well-scoped. Use price velocity (simpler than 5m ADX). Start with 1.5% threshold, use 3-candle average for noise reduction. Catches the 'calm on 15m, volatile on 5m' false positive.
overlap_notes: Complementary to SCOUT-010 (daily trend for directional).
challenge_notes: Calibrate threshold to avoid over-blocking.
next_step: Add 5m velocity check to neutral_scanner scoring.

title: Stuck bot detection watchdog
problem_detected: No watchdog for bots with no progress. 11+ watchdog types but none for stalls.
evidence: watchdog_hub_service.py — confirmed no stuck_bot watchdog
proposed_improvement: Track last_meaningful_activity_at. WARNING at 10min, CRITICAL at 30min. Check price-in-range to avoid false positives.
why_now: Stuck bots waste capital silently.
evaluator_summary: Confirmed gap. Simple addition to existing watchdog infrastructure. Must check price-in-range to distinguish stuck from legitimately idle. Configurable thresholds. Low risk.
overlap_notes: Would feed into SCOUT-006 alert system if built.
challenge_notes: Price-in-range check prevents false positives.
next_step: Add to watchdog_hub with activity tracking.

title: Fill-by-fill audit trail for outage forensics
problem_detected: No fill-level events in audit trail. Outage reconstruction requires 3+ file cross-reference.
evidence: audit_diagnostics_service.py — no order_filled event type
proposed_improvement: Log state transitions first (unique value). Fill logging may duplicate trade_forensics.
why_now: Outage reconstruction pain point.
notes_for_evaluator: Focus on state transitions, not fills.
evaluator_summary: State transition logging is the unique value — fills partially duplicate trade_forensics. Focus on status_change events first. Storage rotation needed (19MB+ already).
overlap_notes: trade_forensics already captures fills.
challenge_notes: Storage rotation. Scope to state transitions first.
next_step: Add status_change events to audit trail.

title: Bounded auto-recovery from error state
problem_detected: Error state is terminal at runtime. Only startup reconciliation handles it.
evidence: runner.py:404 — sets status=error, no recovery path
proposed_improvement: Bounded retry with cooldown for transient errors only. Needs error taxonomy first.
why_now: Manual intervention delay.
evaluator_summary: Confirmed: error is terminal at runtime. Concept sound but prerequisite missing: error classification. Without knowing transient vs structural, auto-recovery is dangerous. DEFERRED until error taxonomy exists.
overlap_notes: Startup reconciliation (Phase 3) shares logic.
challenge_notes: Error taxonomy is the prerequisite. TimeoutError=transient, InsufficientMargin=structural.
next_step: Audit all exception types reaching _persist_bot_cycle_exception. Classify transient vs structural.

title: Extend order ownership snapshot retention to 180 days
problem_detected: 30-day ownership pruning loses attribution for long-held positions.
evidence: order_ownership_service.py:154-168 — 30-day pruning
proposed_improvement: Extend to 180 days with size-based pruning.
why_now: Attribution degradation over time.
evaluator_summary: Grid positions cycle within hours/days. 30-day retention covers 99%+ of cases. No evidence of actual 45+ day holds in this system. 4.2MB → 25MB storage growth not justified. Can revisit if operator reports real attribution gaps.
overlap_notes: None significant.
challenge_notes: No evidence of real-world need.
next_step: Monitor for actual long-hold attribution issues.

title: Proper accounting for unattributed PnL
problem_detected: Unattributed trades dropped from per-symbol totals. 17 references in pnl_service confirmed.
evidence: pnl_service.py — 17 unattributed references confirmed
proposed_improvement: Orphan bucket in symbol_pnl. Include in totals. Skip heuristic attribution for now.
why_now: 20% unattributed = systematically wrong symbol analysis.
evaluator_summary: Confirmed problem. Orphan bucket is the right first step. Skip heuristic attribution (risk of misattribution, same-symbol safety boundary). Display orphan amount for transparency.
overlap_notes: Same-symbol multi-bot is hard safety boundary.
challenge_notes: No heuristic attribution Phase 1. Orphan bucket only.
next_step: Add orphan bucket to symbol_pnl. Display in dashboard.

title: Predictive grid re-centering
problem_detected: Recenter triggers on bounds breach only. One-sided grid wastes capital.
evidence: neutral_grid_service.py:656-1000 — breach-only trigger
proposed_improvement: Predictive shift + dynamic width + hysteresis cooldown.
why_now: Capital inefficiency from delayed recenter.
evaluator_summary: Three features in one (prediction, dynamic width, hysteresis) = architecture project. Thrashing near 20% boundary is fatal flaw. Momentum grid shifting (2026-03-16) already adds trend-awareness. SIMPLER: reduce recenter cooldown (config change, zero code). Only revisit if cooldown reduction insufficient.
overlap_notes: Momentum grid shifting already provides trend-awareness.
challenge_notes: Thrashing. API rate exhaustion. Too many moving parts.
next_step: Try reducing recenter cooldown first (config change).

title: Emergency exit partial fill retry loop
problem_detected: Emergency close issues ONE order, marks position_closed=True without verifying fill. Partial fills leave residual positions that compound with new grid orders.
evidence: neutral_loss_prevention_service.py:642-694 — one close order, marks closed
neutral_loss_prevention_service.py:191-218 — verification detects residual, no retry
guardian_service.py:286-306 — clears flags after 120s without position check
proposed_improvement: After emergency close, verify position size. If residual > 0, retry close up to 3 times. Only mark closed when exchange confirms flat. Escalate to risk_stopped if retries fail.
why_now: Residual positions from failed flattens compound losses. Safety-critical.
notes_for_evaluator: 500ms sync retry vs async next-cycle retry.
evaluator_summary: SAFETY-CRITICAL. Residual positions from partial emergency fills opening new grid on top = compounding losses. The guardian clearing flags after 120s without position verification is dangerous. RECOMMEND: async retry on next cycle (not 500ms sync loop) to avoid blocking runner tick. Verify position via exchange, not cache. Set risk_stopped on 3 failures.
overlap_notes: None. Unique safety gap.
challenge_notes: Use async next-cycle retry, not sync 500ms loop. Verify via exchange.
next_step: Implement retry loop with exchange-verified position check. Fix guardian flag-clearing.

title: Stream freshness clock correctness fix
problem_detected: _note_topic_message() updates freshness timestamp BEFORE handler processes message. Silent handler failures = stale data appears fresh.
evidence: bybit_stream_service.py:1182 — timestamp update before handler
bybit_stream_service.py:2078-2083 — silent None return on JSON errors
proposed_improvement: Move _note_topic_message() to AFTER successful cache update. Add error counter for failed messages.
why_now: Stale data appearing fresh causes incorrect trading decisions.
evaluator_summary: Correctness bug. Single-line-move fix with near-zero risk. Stale data masquerading as fresh affects grid placement, readiness, and risk checks. Only concern: verify handler doesn't depend on timestamp being set before execution. If not, this is the safest high-value fix in the batch.
overlap_notes: None.
challenge_notes: Verify handler doesn't depend on pre-set timestamp.
next_step: Move _note_topic_message() after handler. Add error counter.

title: Neutral mode inventory imbalance ratio detection
problem_detected: Neutral mode checks per-leg caps independently. Bot can be 96% directional while technically within cap. No ratio check.
evidence: neutral_loss_prevention_service.py:804-1020 — per-leg caps only
proposed_improvement: Add imbalance ratio check: abs(long-short)/max(long,short) > threshold → block excess side, trigger partial reduce at extreme.
why_now: Neutral bots becoming directional without detection.
evaluator_summary: Real problem. Neutral bots silently becoming directional in trends. RECOMMEND: Start with imbalance detection + logging only (no auto-reduce Phase 1). Surface imbalance ratio in bot status. Use 0.7 threshold (70% directional). Auto-reduce is Phase 2 after operator sees the pattern. Consider whether recenter should fire earlier as the simpler fix.
overlap_notes: May be partially addressed by better recenter logic.
challenge_notes: Start with detection only, not auto-reduce. Threshold 0.7 is conservative starting point.
next_step: Add imbalance ratio calculation and logging. Surface in bot status. No auto-action Phase 1.

title: Profit protection exit execution in full-live mode
problem_detected: Adaptive profit protection returns 'exit_now' but it's never auto-executed. Advisory only.
evidence: adaptive_profit_protection_service.py:231-490 — exit_now decision
grid_bot_service.py — treats result as informational only
proposed_improvement: Auto-execute exit when decision=exit_now AND mode=full_live. Config toggle default false.
why_now: Identified profit given back because exits not executed.
evaluator_summary: Auto-executing exits is risky if profit protection model has false positives. The rearm guard bug (trapping positions in unexitable state) should be fixed first as it's a standalone correctness issue. Auto-execution needs: (1) false positive rate measurement, (2) 2-consecutive-decision confirmation gate, (3) conflict resolution with NLP/guardian/UPnL stops. DEFERRED until false positive rate is measured and rearm bug is fixed independently.
overlap_notes: Conflicts with NLP, guardian, UPnL stop-loss exit paths.
challenge_notes: False positive rate unknown. Rearm guard bug is independent fix. 2-decision confirmation needed.
next_step: Fix rearm guard bug independently. Measure exit_now false positive rate. Then design auto-execution.

title: TP% profitability validation gate
problem_detected: User can set tp_pct=0.05% while round-trip trading fees are 0.11% (2 * 0.055% taker). This GUARANTEES a loss on every take-profit fill. No validation exists at bot creation or startup. bot_manager_service.py:2064-2074 only rejects tp_pct <= 0. The fee-aware min step exists for grid spacing but there is NO equivalent gate for TP%. Users can also save presets with unprofitable TP%, propagating the problem to every bot created from that preset.
evidence: bot_manager_service.py:2064-2074 — only rejects tp_pct <= 0, no fee check
strategy_config.py:1472-1473 — FAST_EXEC_TAKER_FEE_RATE=0.00055 (0.055%)
Round-trip fees = 0.11%, user can set tp_pct=0.05% → guaranteed loss per TP fill
bot_preset_service.py:224-286 — preset extraction has no TP% range validation
No _compute_fee_aware_min_tp_pct() function exists anywhere
risk_manager_service.py:585-762 — launch validation checks 7 risk metrics but NOT TP profitability
proposed_improvement: Add TP% profitability gate: min_profitable_tp_pct = (2 * FAST_EXEC_TAKER_FEE_RATE) + slippage_buffer + min_profit_margin (e.g., 0.11% + 0.03% + 0.02% = 0.16%). Reject bot creation if tp_pct < min_profitable_tp_pct. Apply same check to preset save. Warn operator in dashboard if existing bot has unprofitable TP%. Fetch actual fee tier from Bybit if available; fall back to conservative hardcoded rate.
why_now: Every bot with unprofitable TP% loses money on every successful take-profit. This is the most direct money-losing misconfiguration possible, and it has zero validation. On small-capital accounts ($35-100), even small per-trade losses compound rapidly.
notes_for_evaluator: Challenge: TP% is sometimes set relative to investment (not price), so the comparison must account for leverage. A 0.05% price move at 10x leverage = 0.5% of investment. Verify that the check compares apples-to-apples. Also consider that maker orders have lower fees (0.02%) — if grid orders fill as maker, the floor should be lower.

title: Same-symbol multi-bot hard prevention at creation
problem_detected: Same-symbol multi-bot ownership is listed as a 'hard safety boundary' in CLAUDE.md, but enforcement is ONLY reactive — triggered during direction-change auto-stop (grid_bot_service.py:1139-1169). No check prevents creating or starting a second bot on the same symbol. Both bots can run simultaneously, placing independent orders. Positions merge on exchange (exchange knows symbol+side, not bot_id). Order ownership attribution breaks. PnL becomes ambiguous. Cleanup of stop_cleanup_pending bots on same symbol can double-close.
evidence: CLAUDE.md — 'Same-symbol multi-bot ownership is a hard safety boundary'
grid_bot_service.py:1139-1169 — _get_shared_symbol_conflict only called during direction-change auto-stop
app.py:4587-4730 — bot creation has no same-symbol check
bot_manager_service.py:1609-2102 — no duplicate symbol validation at creation
risk_manager_service.py:687-702 — checks max_bots_per_symbol but default=2 (allows multi-bot)
Two bots on ETHUSDT both place buy orders → positions merge → attribution impossible
proposed_improvement: Add hard prevention at bot creation: reject if another bot with same symbol exists in status (running, paused, recovering, stop_cleanup_pending). Also reject at bot START if another active bot already owns the symbol. Add explicit exception for Auto-Pilot bots (which rotate symbols). Display blocked reason in dashboard. Consider reducing MAX_BOTS_PER_SYMBOL default from 2 to 1 for non-Auto-Pilot bots.
why_now: This is documented as a hard safety boundary but only softly enforced. The gap between documented safety rule and actual enforcement creates a false sense of security. Any accidental multi-bot same-symbol scenario breaks PnL attribution and can cause double-leverage exposure.
notes_for_evaluator: Challenge: Auto-Pilot bots rotate symbols, so symbol ownership is transient. Must allow Auto-Pilot bots to overlap during rotation transitions. Consider: should the check be at creation (strict) or only at start (allows pre-configuration)? Strict creation check prevents 'staging' bots for quick swap.

title: Move config validation from bot start to bot creation
problem_detected: Critical validation checks (risk limits, leverage caps, grid viability, order sizing, fee-aware min step) only run at bot START, not at bot CREATION. User creates a bot with invalid config (e.g., investment > account equity, leverage > symbol max, grid orders below min notional), saves it successfully, then discovers the failure hours later when they try to start. 7 risk checks in risk_manager.validate_new_bot() and grid viability in analyze_launch() are start-only. Config integrity watchdog only monitors booleans, not numeric ranges.
evidence: bot_manager_service.py:1522-1544 — _validate_and_prepare_launch() calls risk_manager only at START
bot_manager_service.py:1609-2102 — create path validates types but not ranges or cross-params
risk_manager_service.py:585-762 — 7 risk checks run at launch only
bot_manager_service.py:1361-1367 — fee-aware min step computed at launch only
bot_manager_service.py:1369-1511 — analyze_launch() affordability check at START only
config_integrity_watchdog_service.py:6-36 — monitors booleans only, no numeric validation
User creates bot with leverage=100x on 25x-max symbol, saves OK, fails at start
proposed_improvement: Run a lightweight version of validate_new_bot() and analyze_launch() at creation time. Return warnings (not hard blocks) for: investment > equity, leverage > symbol max, grid orders below min notional, TP% below fee floor. Block only for hard violations (investment < 0, leverage < 1). Display validation results in bot creation form as real-time feedback. This catches the most common 'config saved but can't start' scenarios.
why_now: Operator frustration from 'I saved a bot but it won't start' is a repeated friction point. Moving validation earlier reduces wasted time and prevents configs that look valid but aren't. The validation logic already exists — it just needs to be called earlier in the flow.
notes_for_evaluator: Challenge: Some validations require live data (account balance, symbol info) that may not be available during creation. Consider graceful degradation: if live data unavailable, skip that check and note 'will be validated at start'. Also consider: should warnings block creation or just display? Hard blocks reduce flexibility; soft warnings may be ignored.

title: Order flow gate override needs minimum quality floor
problem_detected: Order flow can OVERRIDE entry gate quality/S/R blocks (grid_bot_service.py:5250-5276). When flow_score >= 30 and flow_confidence >= 0.4, entry is forced even if quality gate blocked it. The override clears ALL blocked_by reasons and sets suitable=True. No minimum setup_quality_score floor exists — a setup with quality=10 (terrible) is overridden identically to quality=60 (decent). Structural blocks (breakout chase, price action) are excluded, but quality blocks are not. Order flow can be manipulated via spoofed trades — a coordinated buy sequence triggers flow_score >= 30, which forces entry into a low-quality setup.
evidence: grid_bot_service.py:5250-5276 — flow override code, confirmed by reading actual lines
grid_bot_service.py:5254 — flow_score >= 30 AND flow_confidence >= 0.4 triggers override
grid_bot_service.py:5264 — checks structural_blocks but NOT setup_quality_score
grid_bot_service.py:5271-5274 — clears blocked_by=[], sets suitable=True, marks flow_override=True
grid_bot_service.py:5226 — setup_quality IS computed before override runs, but never checked in override path
Order flow uses 30-second trade window — can be influenced by short bursts of coordinated buys
proposed_improvement: Add minimum quality floor to flow override: only allow override if setup_quality_score >= 35 (or configurable threshold). This prevents flow from forcing entries into truly terrible setups while still allowing it to override marginal S/R blocks on decent setups. Add FLOW_OVERRIDE_MIN_QUALITY config parameter. Log when override is blocked by quality floor for monitoring.
why_now: Flow override is the most powerful entry forcing mechanism in the system (bypasses quality gates). Without a quality floor, any manipulated or noisy flow signal can force entries into setups that the quality gate deliberately rejected. On small-capital accounts, one bad forced entry can consume a significant portion of the loss budget.
notes_for_evaluator: VERIFIED: Read actual code at lines 5249-5276. Override excludes structural blocks (BREAKOUT_CHASE_TOO_FAR, etc.) but NOT quality blocks. setup_quality_score IS computed at line 5226 before the override check, so it's available. The fix is adding one condition: 'and (setup_quality or 0) >= MIN_FLOOR'. Challenge: What should the floor be? Too high (60) makes flow override useless. Too low (20) barely filters anything. Suggest 35 as starting point.

title: Prediction accuracy ledger for confidence calibration
problem_detected: Price prediction service generates direction labels (STRONG_LONG to STRONG_SHORT) and confidence scores (0-100) that directly influence mode selection scoring (±60 points, 30-60% of total weight at grid_bot_service.py:25291). But zero accuracy tracking exists — no comparison of prediction vs. actual price movement after N minutes/hours. Confidence scores are calibrated by internal agreement (how many components agree) but never validated against outcomes. The system cannot determine if 80% confidence actually means 80% correct, or if it means 40% correct. Without calibration data, the ±60 point scoring weight may be too high or too low.
evidence: price_prediction_service.py — grep for 'accuracy','hit_rate','outcome','result' returns 0 matches
grid_bot_service.py:25291 — prediction.predict() called for direction scoring
grid_bot_service.py:25301 — prediction_score capped at ±60 and added to direction score
No storage/prediction_log.json or similar outcome tracking file exists
No test file validates prediction accuracy vs actual price movement
Confidence is calibrated by component agreement (magnitude × agreement weights), not by historical hit rate
proposed_improvement: Add prediction outcome ledger: On each prediction, store {symbol, timestamp, direction, confidence, score, price_at_prediction}. After 15m and 60m, record {price_at_15m, price_at_60m, direction_correct_15m, direction_correct_60m}. Compute rolling accuracy per confidence band (0-30, 30-60, 60-100). Use to calibrate scoring weight: if 80% confidence only predicts correctly 50% of the time, reduce max score contribution. Store in storage/prediction_accuracy.json with daily rollup.
why_now: Predictions contribute 30-60% of mode selection weight. Without accuracy data, this weight is unjustified. If predictions are poorly calibrated, the system systematically enters wrong modes. Accuracy data is the prerequisite for any prediction-based improvement — you can't tune what you can't measure.
notes_for_evaluator: Challenge: Outcome tracking requires storing predictions and checking prices 15-60 minutes later. The runner already has a 10s tick loop — could add a lightweight outcome checker. Storage overhead is modest (one record per symbol per 15 minutes). Consider whether this should be a separate service or embedded in price_prediction_service. Phase 1: just log predictions + outcomes. Phase 2: use accuracy data to auto-calibrate scoring weights.
